terminology_flags.issue: The term 'Julian code' is used but not defined or explained in the document.
terminology_flags.recommendation: Provide a clear definition or explanation of 'Julian code' to ensure understanding and compliance.
terminology_flags.location: • first pack produced after a change in a batch of raw material that would require a Julian code change

terminology_flags.issue: The term 'zone of indecision (ZOI)' is used but not defined or explained in the document.
terminology_flags.recommendation: Include a definition or explanation of 'zone of indecision (ZOI)' to clarify its meaning and application.
terminology_flags.location: The zone of indecision (ZOI) shall be calculated via the Weight and Quantity Control app for each dynamic catch weight and check-weighing system to establish if the variability could lead to non-compliant batches of product.

terminology_flags.issue: The 'Weight and Quantity Control app' is mentioned but not described or defined in the document.
terminology_flags.recommendation: Provide a description or definition of the 'Weight and Quantity Control app' to ensure users understand its purpose and functionality.
terminology_flags.location: The zone of indecision (ZOI) shall be calculated via the Weight and Quantity Control app for each dynamic catch weight and check-weighing system to establish if the variability could lead to non-compliant batches of product.

risk_gaps.location: FSP74b - Online packaging & labelling verification check sheet
risk_gaps.issue: No mention of corrective actions or escalation steps if verification checks fail.
risk_gaps.risk: Failure to address verification check failures could lead to incorrect labelling reaching consumers, posing legal and safety risks.
risk_gaps.recommendation: Include specific corrective actions and escalation steps for failed verification checks.

risk_gaps.location: FSP74c - Dimaco Veri-pack label Check Sheet
risk_gaps.issue: No mention of person responsible for conducting checks.
risk_gaps.risk: Lack of accountability could lead to checks being missed or improperly conducted.
risk_gaps.recommendation: Specify the person or role responsible for conducting the checks.

risk_gaps.issue: No verification method for ensuring pre-coded/labelled packaging is segregated from original packaging.
risk_gaps.risk: Mixing of pre-coded and original packaging could lead to incorrect labelling and traceability issues.
risk_gaps.recommendation: Define a verification method to ensure proper segregation of packaging.

risk_gaps.issue: No mention of how the site ensures product liners and bags are resistant to tearing.
risk_gaps.risk: Tearing could lead to contamination of products, posing safety risks.
risk_gaps.recommendation: Include procedures or tests to verify the tear resistance of product liners and bags.

risk_gaps.issue: No mention of corrective actions if product inspection or testing fails.
risk_gaps.risk: Failure to address inspection or testing failures could lead to unsafe products reaching consumers.
risk_gaps.recommendation: Include specific corrective actions for failed inspections or tests.

risk_gaps.issue: No specific procedure for conducting root cause analysis.
risk_gaps.risk: Without a defined procedure, root cause analysis may be inconsistent or ineffective, leading to repeated errors.
risk_gaps.recommendation: Define a detailed procedure for conducting root cause analysis.

risk_gaps.issue: No mention of who verifies the system is functioning correctly before reducing check frequency.
risk_gaps.risk: Reducing check frequency without proper verification could lead to undetected errors.
risk_gaps.recommendation: Specify the role responsible for verifying system functionality before reducing check frequency.

risk_gaps.issue: No mention of verification method for ensuring ingredient and allergen labelling is correct.
risk_gaps.risk: Incorrect allergen labelling could pose serious health risks to consumers.
risk_gaps.recommendation: Include a verification method for ensuring the accuracy of ingredient and allergen labelling.

risk_gaps.issue: No mention of how risks of incorrect packaging/labelling are identified and assessed.
risk_gaps.risk: Without risk assessment, potential issues may not be identified or mitigated.
risk_gaps.recommendation: Include a risk assessment process for identifying and evaluating risks related to packaging and labelling.

risk_gaps.issue: No mention of calibration or maintenance procedures for the vision system.
risk_gaps.risk: Improperly calibrated or maintained systems could lead to incorrect labelling checks.
risk_gaps.recommendation: Include calibration and maintenance procedures for the vision system.

specifying_flags.recommendation: Specify the exact frequency for conducting fail-safe checks, e.g., every 30 minutes, hourly, etc.

specifying_flags.current_text: labelling area to be free from other packaging items
specifying_flags.recommendation: Provide measurable criteria for 'free from other packaging items', e.g., no more than 5 items present.
Specify the exact colour or contrast level required for product liners and bags.
Define the specific procedures, facilities, and standards to be used for product inspection and testing.
Specify the new frequency if the 30-minute check is reduced, e.g., every 60 minutes.
Specify the exact backup system requirements, e.g., daily backups with offsite storage.
Specify the conditions or criteria under which risk reduction is possible.
Specify the unit of time, e.g., every 30 minutes.
Define what constitutes a significant deviation, e.g., more than 10% change in dimensions.
Specify the exact frequency for conducting fail-safe checks.
Provide specific criteria or standards for what constitutes adequate controls.
Specify the exact colour or range of colours that are considered appropriate.
Define the specific procedures that are considered appropriate.
Specify the new frequency or conditions under which the frequency can be reduced.
Define the specific conditions or criteria that determine when risk can be reduced or eliminated.
Clarify if '30 mins' refers to 30 minutes exactly or if there is an acceptable range or tolerance.
Define what constitutes a significant deviation in terms of specific measurements or criteria.

sequencing_flags.issue: Pre-coded/labelled packaging is not segregated from original packaging storage location before checks.
sequencing_flags.impact: Potential for cross-contact and incorrect labelling.
sequencing_flags.recommendation: Ensure pre-coded/labelled packaging is segregated from original packaging storage location before checks are conducted.

sequencing_flags.issue: Checks for the first pack produced after any break in production are listed after checks for every 30 minutes during production.
sequencing_flags.impact: Potential for missed errors in the first pack after production changes.
sequencing_flags.recommendation: Move checks for the first pack produced after any break in production before the 30-minute interval checks.

sequencing_flags.issue: Fail-safe checks are required at the start and end of production, but there is no mention of checks after maintenance or repairs.
sequencing_flags.impact: Risk of undetected errors after maintenance or repairs.
sequencing_flags.recommendation: Include fail-safe checks immediately after any repairs or maintenance conducted on the line.

formatting_flags.recommendation: Ensure the document title is clearly defined and separated from the rest of the text.
Break down the introduction into smaller paragraphs or bullet points for better readability.
Separate responsibilities by role into a list format for clarity.
Break down the paragraph into bullet points for each control measure for better readability.
Separate the points into a list format to improve readability.
Break down the paragraph into bullet points or numbered steps for clarity.
Separate the different requirements into bullet points for better readability.
Break down the scope into smaller paragraphs or bullet points for clarity.
Separate the testing frequencies into a list format for better readability.
Break down the paragraph into bullet points for each condition that requires re-evaluation.
Ensure all mandatory sections such as Scope, Responsibilities, CCPs, and Related Documents are clearly defined and separated.

compliance_flags.recommendation: Ensure documented corrective actions are in place for labelling or date coding errors, including root cause analysis.
Include a process to verify that ingredient and allergen labelling is correct based on the product recipe and ingredient specifications.
Add record forms for verification checks of automated label verification and date coding equipment.
Implement a workflow to ensure allergen information is reviewed and updated whenever changes occur to product recipe, process, or raw materials.
Ensure that all amendments to the controlled database for automated label verification are signed off by authorized personnel.
